forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KT9FY7SBW0MVVAZ4A1WZP4SS
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: b380
+project: plugin-arch
+title: 'SDK: wire this.<server>.on(event, cb) on the dispatch proxy (replace inert reservedHandler)'
+---
+The TypeScript half of the SDK event-subscription API. Turns the reserved-but-inert `on`/`subscribe` proxy names (crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:706-728, `reservedHandler()` returns a no-op) into the real per-server subscribe surface. Depends on the host card landing the bridge→isolate delivery path + subscribe/unsubscribe envelopes.
+
+## Chosen surface (user-approved)
+```ts
+const off = this.commands.on("executed", (params) => { /* react */ });
+off();            // optional explicit teardown
+```
+- `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` → MCP method `notifications/<server>/<event>` (e.g. commands+executed → `notifications/commands/executed`).
+- Returns an unsubscribe function (an Obsidian-style EventRef). Auto-cleaned on unload via the per-plugin ledger; explicit `off()` optional.
+
+## Scope (sdk/plugin.ts)
+1. Add `Transport.subscribe(server, event, callback)` (+ unsubscribe) near the existing `callPath`/`callbackDispatch` members (Transport doc ~plugin.ts:462-476). It marshals `callback` via the existing `marshalCallbacks` and sends the new `notifications/subscribe` envelope; returns the callback id so the unsubscribe fn can send `notifications/unsubscribe`.
+2. In `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get` (plugin.ts:747-756): for `on`/`subscribe`, return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted `server`. Decide `off`/`once` semantics: `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire; `off` may stay an alias/teardown. Keep `then` reported absent. Keep `RESERVED` only for names that must NOT extend the path.
+3. The returned unsubscribe fn sends the unsubscribe envelope and disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`).
+4. Replace the stale doc comments ("intentionally inert" / "not part of this SDK task") with the real contract. If a helper mirrors `registerCommands` ergonomics, keep it minimal — the proxy `.on()` is the primary surface.
+
+## Tests
+- Unit (deno/TS or via the Rust harness): `.on()` produces a `notifications/subscribe` envelope carrying a `$callback` marker + the assembled method; the returned fn produces `notifications/unsubscribe`.
+- Real end-to-end coverage lands in the dependent integration-test card.
+
+## Acceptance
+A plugin's `load()` can call `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` and receive callback invocations when `notifications/commands/executed` is published; `off()` stops them.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
 project: plugin-arch
 title: 'SDK: wire this.<server>.on(event, cb) on the dispatch proxy (replace inert reservedHandler)'
 ---
-The TypeScript half of the SDK event-subscription API. Turns the reserved-but-inert `on`/`subscribe` proxy names (crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:706-728, `reservedHandler()` returns a no-op) into the real per-server subscribe surface. Depends on the host card landing the bridge→isolate delivery path + subscribe/unsubscribe envelopes.
+The ergonomic proxy sugar for the SDK event-subscription API. The low-level wire primitive (`Transport.subscribe`/`unsubscribe`, callback marshalling, the `subscribe`/`unsubscribe` envelopes, host registry + pump) ALREADY LANDED with the host card (01KT9FY7SBW0MVVAZ4A1WZP4SS) so that card was end-to-end testable. This card builds the `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` surface on top of that primitive and replaces the inert reservedHandler.

 ## Chosen surface (user-approved)
 ```ts
@@ -15,18 +15,16 @@
 const off = this.commands.on("executed", (params) => { /* react */ });
 off();            // optional explicit teardown
 ```
-- `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` → MCP method `notifications/<server>/<event>` (e.g. commands+executed → `notifications/commands/executed`).
-- Returns an unsubscribe function (an Obsidian-style EventRef). Auto-cleaned on unload via the per-plugin ledger; explicit `off()` optional.
+- `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` → assemble MCP method `notifications/<server>/<event>` and call `transport.subscribe(method, cb)` (already implemented). Return an unsubscribe function that calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` AND disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`).

 ## Scope (sdk/plugin.ts)
-1. Add `Transport.subscribe(server, event, callback)` (+ unsubscribe) near the existing `callPath`/`callbackDispatch` members (Transport doc ~plugin.ts:462-476). It marshals `callback` via the existing `marshalCallbacks` and sends the new `notifications/subscribe` envelope; returns the callback id so the unsubscribe fn can send `notifications/unsubscribe`.
-2. In `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get` (plugin.ts:747-756): for `on`/`subscribe`, return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted `server`. Decide `off`/`once` semantics: `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire; `off` may stay an alias/teardown. Keep `then` reported absent. Keep `RESERVED` only for names that must NOT extend the path.
-3. The returned unsubscribe fn sends the unsubscribe envelope and disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`).
-4. Replace the stale doc comments ("intentionally inert" / "not part of this SDK task") with the real contract. If a helper mirrors `registerCommands` ergonomics, keep it minimal — the proxy `.on()` is the primary surface.
+1. In `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get` (plugin.ts:747-756): for `on`/`subscribe` return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted `server`, assembling `notifications/${server}/${event}` and delegating to `this.__transport.subscribe(...)`. `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire; decide `off` semantics (alias/no-op handle). Keep `then` reported absent.
+2. The returned unsubscribe fn calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` and disposes the local callback table entry so `off()` is a full teardown (host stops delivery + isolate frees the fn).
+3. Replace the stale doc comments ("intentionally inert" / "not part of this SDK task") on the RESERVED handler with the real contract. Keep `RESERVED` only for names that must not extend the dispatch path (notably `then`).

 ## Tests
-- Unit (deno/TS or via the Rust harness): `.on()` produces a `notifications/subscribe` envelope carrying a `$callback` marker + the assembled method; the returned fn produces `notifications/unsubscribe`.
-- Real end-to-end coverage lands in the dependent integration-test card.
+- Verify `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` assembles `notifications/commands/executed` and round-trips through the (already-tested) transport primitive — extend the real-pipeline test (01KT9FZ8GZ) to drive `.on()` instead of the raw `this.__transport.subscribe`.
+- `off()` stops delivery and disposes the local callback.

 ## Acceptance
-A plugin's `load()` can call `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` and receive callback invocations when `notifications/commands/executed` is published; `off()` stops them.
\ No newline at end of file
+A plugin calls `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` (no raw transport access) and receives callback invocations on `notifications/commands/executed`; `off()` stops them and frees the isolate callback.
\ No newline at end of file

+The TypeScript half of the SDK event-subscription API. Turns the reserved-but-inert `on`/`subscribe` proxy names (crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:706-728, `reservedHandler()` returns a no-op) into the real per-server subscribe surface. Depends on the host card landing the bridge→isolate delivery path + subscribe/unsubscribe envelopes.

 ## Chosen surface (user-approved)
 ```ts
@@ -15,16 +15,18 @@
 const off = this.commands.on("executed", (params) => { /* react */ });

 ```
-- `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` → assemble MCP method `notifications/<server>/<event>` and call `transport.subscribe(method, cb)` (already implemented). Return an unsubscribe function that calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` AND disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`).
+- `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` → MCP method `notifications/<server>/<event>` (e.g. commands+executed → `notifications/commands/executed`).
+- Returns an unsubscribe function (an Obsidian-style EventRef). Auto-cleaned on unload via the per-plugin ledger; explicit `off()` optional.

 ## Scope (sdk/plugin.ts)
-1. In `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get` (plugin.ts:747-756): for `on`/`subscribe` return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted `server`, assembling `notifications/${server}/${event}` and delegating to `this.__transport.subscribe(...)`. `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire; decide `off` semantics (alias/no-op handle). Keep `then` reported absent.
-2. The returned unsubscribe fn calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` and disposes the local callback table entry so `off()` is a full teardown (host stops delivery + isolate frees the fn).
-3. Replace the stale doc comments ("intentionally inert" / "not part of this SDK task") on the RESERVED handler with the real contract. Keep `RESERVED` only for names that must not extend the dispatch path (notably `then`).
+1. Add `Transport.subscribe(server, event, callback)` (+ unsubscribe) near the existing `callPath`/`callbackDispatch` members (Transport doc ~plugin.ts:462-476). It marshals `callback` via the existing `marshalCallbacks` and sends the new `notifications/subscribe` envelope; returns the callback id so the unsubscribe fn can send `notifications/unsubscribe`.
+2. In `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get` (plugin.ts:747-756): for `on`/`subscribe`, return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted `server`. Decide `off`/`once` semantics: `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire; `off` may stay an alias/teardown. Keep `then` reported absent. Keep `RESERVED` only for names that must NOT extend the path.
+3. The returned unsubscribe fn sends the unsubscribe envelope and disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`).
+4. Replace the stale doc comments ("intentionally inert" / "not part of this SDK task") with the real contract. If a helper mirrors `registerCommands` ergonomics, keep it minimal — the proxy `.on()` is the primary surface.

 ## Tests
-- Verify `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` assembles `notifications/commands/executed` and round-trips through the (already-tested) transport primitive — extend the real-pipeline test (01KT9FZ8GZ) to drive `.on()` instead of the raw `this.__transport.subscribe`.
-- `off()` stops delivery and disposes the local callback.
+- Unit (deno/TS or via the Rust harness): `.on()` produces a `notifications/subscribe` envelope carrying a `$callback` marker + the assembled method; the returned fn produces `notifications/unsubscribe`.
+- Real end-to-end coverage lands in the dependent integration-test card.

 ## Acceptance
-A plugin calls `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` (no raw transport access) and receives callback invocations on `notifications/commands/executed`; `off()` stops them and frees the isolate callback.
\ No newline at end of file
+A plugin's `load()` can call `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` and receive callback invocations when `notifications/commands/executed` is published; `off()` stops them.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,28 +3,31 @@
 - claude-code
 depends_on:
 - 01KT9FY7SBW0MVVAZ4A1WZP4SS
+- 01KT9JTDE3EX2BQNQ4F3HMZYTP
+- 01KT9JV0N1RF0MMEBRZV4A6F3J
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: b380
 project: plugin-arch
 title: 'SDK: wire this.<server>.on(event, cb) on the dispatch proxy (replace inert reservedHandler)'
 ---
-The ergonomic proxy sugar for the SDK event-subscription API. The low-level wire primitive (`Transport.subscribe`/`unsubscribe`, callback marshalling, the `subscribe`/`unsubscribe` envelopes, host registry + pump) ALREADY LANDED with the host card (01KT9FY7SBW0MVVAZ4A1WZP4SS) so that card was end-to-end testable. This card builds the `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` surface on top of that primitive and replaces the inert reservedHandler.
+The ergonomic `.on()` proxy surface, METADATA-DRIVEN. The low-level wire primitive (`Transport.subscribe`/`unsubscribe`, host registry + pump) already landed with the host card (01KT9FY7SBW0MVVAZ4A1WZP4SS). The notification vocabulary is declared per-service in `_meta["io.swissarmyhammer/notifications"]` (cards 01KT9JTDE3 macro + 01KT9JV0N1 declarations). This card builds `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` resolving the event against that `_meta` — exactly mirroring how operations resolve — and replaces the inert reservedHandler.

 ## Chosen surface (user-approved)
 ```ts
-const off = this.commands.on("executed", (params) => { /* react */ });
-off();            // optional explicit teardown
+const off = this.commands.on("executed", (params) => { /* params typed from the decl */ });
+off();   // unsubscribe + dispose
 ```
-- `this.<server>.on(event, cb)` → assemble MCP method `notifications/<server>/<event>` and call `transport.subscribe(method, cb)` (already implemented). Return an unsubscribe function that calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` AND disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`).

 ## Scope (sdk/plugin.ts)
-1. In `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get` (plugin.ts:747-756): for `on`/`subscribe` return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted `server`, assembling `notifications/${server}/${event}` and delegating to `this.__transport.subscribe(...)`. `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire; decide `off` semantics (alias/no-op handle). Keep `then` reported absent.
-2. The returned unsubscribe fn calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` and disposes the local callback table entry so `off()` is a full teardown (host stops delivery + isolate frees the fn).
-3. Replace the stale doc comments ("intentionally inert" / "not part of this SDK task") on the RESERVED handler with the real contract. Keep `RESERVED` only for names that must not extend the dispatch path (notably `then`).
+1. **Read `io.swissarmyhammer/notifications` from `_meta`** — add `NOTIFICATIONS_META_KEY` + a `notificationsOf(tool)` reader mirroring `operationsOf` (plugin.ts:711) and a `lookupNotification(server, event) -> method` mirroring `lookupOp` (plugin.ts:725). Resolve via the server's cached tool defs (same `this.tools(server)` cache the dispatch path uses).
+2. **Wire `on`/`subscribe` in `makeDispatcher`'s Proxy `get`** (plugin.ts:747-756): return a real `(event, cb) => off` bound to the rooted server. It resolves `event → method` via the notifications `_meta`; throws `UnknownNotification` listing the valid events on a miss (mirror `UnknownOperation`); calls `transport.subscribe(method, cb)` (already implemented). `once` wraps to auto-unsubscribe after first fire. Keep `then` reported absent; keep `RESERVED` only for names that must not extend the path.
+3. **Return an off handle** that calls `transport.unsubscribe(method, id)` AND disposes the local callback (`__sahDisposeCallback`) — full teardown (host stops delivery + isolate frees the fn). Auto-cleans on unload via the ledger, so `off()` is optional.
+4. Replace the stale "intentionally inert / not part of this SDK task" doc comments on the reserved handler with the real contract.

 ## Tests
-- Verify `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` assembles `notifications/commands/executed` and round-trips through the (already-tested) transport primitive — extend the real-pipeline test (01KT9FZ8GZ) to drive `.on()` instead of the raw `this.__transport.subscribe`.
+- `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` resolves to `notifications/commands/executed` from `_meta` and round-trips through the transport primitive (extend the real-pipeline test 01KT9FZ8GZ to drive `.on()` against a service that declares the notification).
+- Unknown event throws `UnknownNotification` listing valid events.
 - `off()` stops delivery and disposes the local callback.

 ## Acceptance
-A plugin calls `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` (no raw transport access) and receives callback invocations on `notifications/commands/executed`; `off()` stops them and frees the isolate callback.
\ No newline at end of file
+A plugin calls `this.commands.on("executed", cb)` (no raw transport access), resolved against the service's declared notifications; receives invocations on `notifications/commands/executed`; `off()` stops them and frees the isolate callback; an undeclared event errors clearly.
\ No newline at end of file
